title: Introduction to tidyEmoji
text: Emoji everywhere modern text — social-media posts, product reviews, chat support logs, survey free-text — carry information plain words . Yet summarising emoji corpus surprisingly awkward. Unicode interact cleanly regular expressions, every code point emoji, single visible emoji often built several code points joined together. Counting “many posts contain emoji” “emoji common” hand quickly becomes painful. tidyEmoji removes friction. provides small family verbs take data frame name text column, return tidy data frames drop straight dplyr/ggplot2 workflow: Two design choices worth highlighting: Grapheme-aware detection. Detection performed whole grapheme clusters, skin-tone modifiers (👍🏽) zero-width-joiner sequences family emoji (👨‍👩‍👧‍👦) treated single emoji rather split component parts. illustrated extraction section. Tidy default. Every verb returns tibble, follows verb(data, text_column) convention, supports unquoted column names, functions compose naturally pipe.
code: library(tidyEmoji) library(dplyr) library(ggplot2)

title: Introduction to tidyEmoji
text: Throughout vignette use sample text collected Atlanta, Georgia. data happens come social-media corpus, nothing specific platform — data frame text column . actual text lives full_text column, column pass tidyEmoji verb.
code: ata_tweets <- readr::read_csv("ata_tweets.csv", show_col_types = FALSE) ata_tweets #> # A tibble: 2,000 × 1 #>    full_text                                                                     #>    <chr>                                                                         #>  1 "Was Justin Bieber ever at any BLM March/protest this past summer? Or ever?"  #>  2 "Whole Family gone and I’m stuck here in Brunswick 😭"                        #>  3 "38 years old and I still get distracted while cleaning my room. \n\nJust in… #>  4 "This time last year I was blasting fever nonstop by Wizkid 😭"               #>  5 "Kanye is a just a black man with a lot of confidence and y’all tear him dow… #>  6 "Feel like being my inner self today"                                         #>  7 "Peep toe boots irk my sole."                                                 #>  8 "Nah the biggest naruto fans be trying call one piece long like... nigga?"    #>  9 "if my shoes don’t arrive by next Friday imma be pissed"                      #> 10 "Phone dry asf 🙄"                                                            #> # ℹ 1,990 more rows

title: Introduction to tidyEmoji
text: emoji_summary() answers first question one usually asks new corpus: much emoji ? returns one-row tibble number entries contain least one emoji total number entries. entry counted regardless many emoji holds. , 560 2,000 entries (28%) contain least one emoji.
code: summary_tbl <- ata_tweets %>%   emoji_summary(full_text)  summary_tbl #> # A tibble: 1 × 2 #>   n_with_emoji n_total #>          <int>   <int> #> 1          560    2000

title: Introduction to tidyEmoji
text: emoji_filter() keeps rows whose text contains least one emoji, preserving every original column. useful want compare emoji-bearing emoji-free text, restrict analysis emoji subset. (emoji_tweets() synonym retained backward compatibility.)
code: ata_tweets %>%   emoji_filter(full_text) #> # A tibble: 560 × 1 #>    full_text                                                                     #>    <chr>                                                                         #>  1 Whole Family gone and I’m stuck here in Brunswick 😭                          #>  2 This time last year I was blasting fever nonstop by Wizkid 😭                 #>  3 Phone dry asf 🙄                                                              #>  4 When I gave my life to Christ, I was able to see people as imperfect, but st… #>  5 Everyone needs self care days❤️                                                #>  6 R u sears rn 🤦🏽‍♀️                                                               #>  7 Gucci wit some dope runnas head huncho top gunna u a sto runna😭              #>  8 i’m thinking insomnia cause they got this caramel apple pie cookie. 😋        #>  9 i deadass listen to the music everyday lmao🥲                                 #> 10 Im ona block where ya barley can be at  if you try get shot down 😈           #> # ℹ 550 more rows

title: Introduction to tidyEmoji
text: tidyEmoji offers three complementary ways pull emoji text, depending shape output want.

title: Introduction to tidyEmoji
text: emoji_extract_nest() leaves data unchanged except added list-column, .emoji_unicode, holding emoji found row. original data structure preserved, makes convenient intermediate step.
code: ata_tweets %>%   emoji_extract_nest(full_text) %>%   select(full_text, .emoji_unicode) #> # A tibble: 2,000 × 2 #>    full_text                                                      .emoji_unicode #>    <chr>                                                          <list>         #>  1 "Was Justin Bieber ever at any BLM March/protest this past su… <chr [0]>      #>  2 "Whole Family gone and I’m stuck here in Brunswick 😭"         <chr [1]>      #>  3 "38 years old and I still get distracted while cleaning my ro… <chr [0]>      #>  4 "This time last year I was blasting fever nonstop by Wizkid 😭… <chr [1]>      #>  5 "Kanye is a just a black man with a lot of confidence and y’a… <chr [0]>      #>  6 "Feel like being my inner self today"                          <chr [0]>      #>  7 "Peep toe boots irk my sole."                                  <chr [0]>      #>  8 "Nah the biggest naruto fans be trying call one piece long li… <chr [0]>      #>  9 "if my shoes don’t arrive by next Friday imma be pissed"       <chr [0]>      #> 10 "Phone dry asf 🙄"                                             <chr [1]>      #> # ℹ 1,990 more rows

title: Introduction to tidyEmoji
text: emoji_extract_unnest() returns long, tidy table one row per (entry, emoji) pair: .row_number records position entry data, .emoji_unicode emoji, .emoji_count many times emoji occurs entry. Entries without emoji dropped. can use plot many emoji emoji-bearing entry contains:  overwhelming majority emoji-bearing entries carry just one emoji, long, thin tail emoji-heavy entries.
code: emoji_per_tweet <- ata_tweets %>%   emoji_extract_unnest(full_text)  emoji_per_tweet #> # A tibble: 697 × 3 #>    .row_number .emoji_unicode .emoji_count #>          <int> <chr>                 <int> #>  1           2 😭                        1 #>  2           4 😭                        1 #>  3          10 🙄                        1 #>  4          15 😂                        1 #>  5          17 ❤️                         1 #>  6          30 🤦🏽‍♀️                        1 #>  7          31 😭                        1 #>  8          33 😋                        1 #>  9          42 🥲                        1 #> 10          45 😈                        1 #> # ℹ 687 more rows emoji_per_tweet %>%   group_by(.row_number) %>%   summarise(n_emoji = sum(.emoji_count)) %>%   ggplot(aes(n_emoji)) +   geom_bar() +   scale_x_continuous(breaks = seq(1, 15)) +   labs(x = "Number of emoji in the entry",        y = "Number of entries",        title = "Most emoji-bearing entries contain a single emoji")

title: Introduction to tidyEmoji
text: emoji_tokens() produces “one row per emoji occurrence” table — emoji analogue tidy-text token table. keeps original columns adds glyph (.emoji) together name (.emoji_name), category (.emoji_category) sentiment score (.emoji_sentiment). single call gives everything needed counting, joining plotting.
code: ata_tweets %>%   emoji_tokens(full_text) #> # A tibble: 901 × 5 #>    full_text                 .emoji .emoji_name .emoji_category .emoji_sentiment #>    <chr>                     <chr>  <chr>       <chr>                      <dbl> #>  1 Whole Family gone and I’… 😭     loudly cry… Smileys & Emot…          -0.0934 #>  2 This time last year I wa… 😭     loudly cry… Smileys & Emot…          -0.0934 #>  3 Phone dry asf 🙄          🙄     face with … Smileys & Emot…          NA      #>  4 When I gave my life to C… 😂     face with … Smileys & Emot…           0.221  #>  5 Everyone needs self care… ❤️      red heart   Smileys & Emot…           0.746  #>  6 R u sears rn 🤦🏽‍♀️           🤦🏽‍♀️     woman face… People & Body            NA      #>  7 Gucci wit some dope runn… 😭     loudly cry… Smileys & Emot…          -0.0934 #>  8 i’m thinking insomnia ca… 😋     face savor… Smileys & Emot…           0.634  #>  9 i deadass listen to the … 🥲     smiling fa… Smileys & Emot…          NA      #> 10 Im ona block where ya ba… 😈     smiling fa… Smileys & Emot…           0.268  #> # ℹ 891 more rows

title: Introduction to tidyEmoji
text: Modern emoji frequently composed several code points: base emoji plus skin-tone modifier, several emoji joined zero-width joiners. tidyEmoji detects emoji level grapheme clusters, stay intact. example contains exactly two emoji — one family one thumbs-— tidyEmoji counts rather splitting family four people separating thumb skin tone:
code: demo <- data.frame(   text = c("our family \U0001F468‍\U0001F469‍\U0001F467‍\U0001F466",            "great work \U0001F44D\U0001F3FD") )  demo %>%   emoji_extract_unnest(text) #> # A tibble: 2 × 3 #>   .row_number .emoji_unicode .emoji_count #>         <int> <chr>                 <int> #> 1           1 👨‍👩‍👧‍👦                        1 #> 2           2 👍🏽                        1

title: Introduction to tidyEmoji
text: emoji_frequency() counts often emoji appears across whole text column (entry containing emoji twice contributes 2) returns result sorted descending count, annotated emoji’s name, shortcode category.
code: ata_tweets %>%   emoji_frequency(full_text) #> # A tibble: 188 × 5 #>    emoji name                          shortcode                     group     n #>    <chr> <chr>                         <chr>                         <chr> <int> #>  1 😂    face with tears of joy        joy                           Smil…   160 #>  2 😭    loudly crying face            sob                           Smil…    98 #>  3 😩    weary face                    weary                         Smil…    34 #>  4 🤣    rolling on the floor laughing rofl                          Smil…    34 #>  5 🥺    pleading face                 pleading_face                 Smil…    29 #>  6 🙄    face with rolling eyes        roll_eyes                     Smil…    22 #>  7 🥴    woozy face                    woozy_face                    Smil…    21 #>  8 💯    hundred points                100                           Smil…    20 #>  9 😍    smiling face with heart-eyes  heart_eyes                    Smil…    20 #> 10 🥰    smiling face with hearts      smiling_face_with_three_hear… Smil…    20 #> # ℹ 178 more rows

"),          emoji_name = forcats::fct_reorder(emoji_name, n)) %>%   ggplot(aes(n, emoji_name, fill = emoji_category)) +   geom_col() +   labs(x = "Count",        y = NULL,        fill = "Category",        title = "The 20 most frequent emoji") ata_tweets %>%   top_n_emojis(full_text, n = 10) %>%   mutate(emoji_name = stringr::str_replace_all(emoji_name, "_", " "),          emoji_name = forcats::fct_reorder(emoji_name, n)) %>%   ggplot(aes(n, emoji_name, fill = emoji_category)) +   geom_col() +   labs(x = "Count", y = NULL, fill = "Category",        title = "The 10 most frequent emoji")

title: Introduction to tidyEmoji
text: Unicode standard organises emoji 10 categories (see ?category_unicode_crosswalk). emoji_categorize() keeps emoji-bearing rows adds .emoji_category column listing distinct categories present row, separated | row spans one. can tally common category combinations:  count 10 individual categories rather combinations, split .emoji_category strings | tidyr::separate_rows():  “Smileys & Emotion” dominates, followed “People & Body”. Note entry spanning several categories counted , counts can exceed number emoji-bearing entries.
code: ata_emoji_category <- ata_tweets %>%   emoji_categorize(full_text) %>%   select(.emoji_category)  ata_emoji_category #> # A tibble: 560 × 1 #>    .emoji_category   #>    <chr>             #>  1 Smileys & Emotion #>  2 Smileys & Emotion #>  3 Smileys & Emotion #>  4 Smileys & Emotion #>  5 Smileys & Emotion #>  6 People & Body     #>  7 Smileys & Emotion #>  8 Smileys & Emotion #>  9 Smileys & Emotion #> 10 Smileys & Emotion #> # ℹ 550 more rows ata_emoji_category %>%   count(.emoji_category, sort = TRUE) %>%   filter(n > 20) %>%   mutate(.emoji_category = forcats::fct_reorder(.emoji_category, n)) %>%   ggplot(aes(n, .emoji_category)) +   geom_col() +   labs(x = "Number of entries", y = NULL,        title = "Most common emoji category combinations") ata_emoji_category %>%   tidyr::separate_rows(.emoji_category, sep = "\\|") %>%   count(.emoji_category, sort = TRUE) %>%   mutate(.emoji_category = forcats::fct_reorder(.emoji_category, n)) %>%   ggplot(aes(n, .emoji_category)) +   geom_col() +   labs(x = "Number of entries", y = NULL,        title = "Emoji category usage")

title: Introduction to tidyEmoji
text: Emoji strong sentiment signal, emoji_sentiment() surfaces directly. adds .emoji_n (number emoji entry), .emoji_n_scored (number appear lexicon), .emoji_sentiment (mean sentiment scored emoji, -1 +1). Scores come bundled emoji_sentiment_lexicon (described ); entries emoji, whose emoji lexicon, receive NA.
code: ata_sentiment <- ata_tweets %>%   emoji_sentiment(full_text)  ata_sentiment %>%   select(.emoji_n, .emoji_sentiment) #> # A tibble: 2,000 × 2 #>    .emoji_n .emoji_sentiment #>       <int>            <dbl> #>  1        0          NA      #>  2        1          -0.0934 #>  3        0          NA      #>  4        1          -0.0934 #>  5        0          NA      #>  6        0          NA      #>  7        0          NA      #>  8        0          NA      #>  9        0          NA      #> 10        1          NA      #> # ℹ 1,990 more rows

title: Introduction to tidyEmoji
text: Looking across entries contain least one scored emoji:  typical social-media text, emoji sentiment leans strongly positive.
code: ata_sentiment %>%   filter(!is.na(.emoji_sentiment)) %>%   ggplot(aes(.emoji_sentiment)) +   geom_histogram(binwidth = 0.1) +   labs(x = "Mean emoji sentiment",        y = "Number of entries",        title = "Emoji sentiment skews positive")

title: Introduction to tidyEmoji
text: emoji_tokens() attaches sentiment score every emoji occurrence, can summarise average sentiment category couple lines:
code: ata_tweets %>%   emoji_tokens(full_text) %>%   group_by(.emoji_category) %>%   summarise(mean_sentiment = mean(.emoji_sentiment, na.rm = TRUE),             n_scored = sum(!is.na(.emoji_sentiment))) %>%   filter(n_scored > 0) %>%   mutate(.emoji_category = forcats::fct_reorder(.emoji_category, mean_sentiment)) %>%   ggplot(aes(mean_sentiment, .emoji_category)) +   geom_col() +   labs(x = "Mean sentiment", y = NULL,        title = "Average emoji sentiment by category")

title: Introduction to tidyEmoji
text: scores come emoji_sentiment_lexicon, Emoji Sentiment Ranking Kralj Novak et al. (2015), computed around 70,000 tweets annotated 13 European languages. can work directly — instance, find positive negative reasonably common emoji:
code: emoji_sentiment_lexicon %>%   filter(occurrences >= 500) %>%   slice_max(sentiment_score, n = 8) %>%   select(emoji, unicode_name, occurrences, sentiment_score) #>   emoji         unicode_name occurrences sentiment_score #> 1     ❤    HEAVY BLACK HEART        8050       0.7460870 #> 2    💞     REVOLVING HEARTS         687       0.7423581 #> 3    🎉         PARTY POPPER        1125       0.7395556 #> 4    💃               DANCER        1344       0.7358631 #> 5    💙           BLUE HEART         912       0.7324561 #> 6    💖      SPARKLING HEART        1263       0.7133808 #> 7    💛         YELLOW HEART         602       0.7126246 #> 8    😘 FACE THROWING A KISS        3648       0.7017544  emoji_sentiment_lexicon %>%   filter(occurrences >= 500) %>%   slice_min(sentiment_score, n = 8) %>%   select(emoji, unicode_name, occurrences, sentiment_score) #>   emoji       unicode_name occurrences sentiment_score #> 1    😒      UNAMUSED FACE        1385     -0.37472924 #> 2    😩         WEARY FACE        1808     -0.36836283 #> 3    🔫             PISTOL         604     -0.19536424 #> 4    😡       POUTING FACE         756     -0.17328042 #> 5    😔       PENSIVE FACE        1205     -0.14605809 #> 6    😞  DISAPPOINTED FACE         532     -0.11842105 #> 7    😭 LOUDLY CRYING FACE        5526     -0.09337676 #> 8    😴      SLEEPING FACE         718     -0.08077994

title: Introduction to tidyEmoji
text: Valence (negative↔︎positive) one affective dimension. emoji_emotion() goes , scoring entry’s emoji across eight Plutchik emotions (anger, anticipation, disgust, fear, joy, sadness, surprise, trust) using bundled EmoTag1200 lexicon (Shoeb & de Melo, 2020). Scores [0, 1]. quick way read result dominant emotion per entry: emotion scores join codepoint-normalised key sentiment, emoji carrying U+FE0F variation selector resolve correctly.
code: ata_emotion <- ata_tweets %>%   emoji_emotion(full_text)  ata_emotion %>%   select(.emoji_joy, .emoji_trust, .emoji_anger, .emoji_n) #> # A tibble: 2,000 × 4 #>    .emoji_joy .emoji_trust .emoji_anger .emoji_n #>         <dbl>        <dbl>        <dbl>    <int> #>  1         NA        NA           NA           0 #>  2          0         0.08         0.22        1 #>  3         NA        NA           NA           0 #>  4          0         0.08         0.22        1 #>  5         NA        NA           NA           0 #>  6         NA        NA           NA           0 #>  7         NA        NA           NA           0 #>  8         NA        NA           NA           0 #>  9         NA        NA           NA           0 #> 10         NA        NA           NA           1 #> # ℹ 1,990 more rows ata_tweets %>%   emoji_emotion_label(full_text) %>%   count(.emoji_emotion, sort = TRUE) #> # A tibble: 9 × 2 #>   .emoji_emotion     n #>   <chr>          <int> #> 1 NA              1639 #> 2 joy              166 #> 3 sadness          115 #> 4 anticipation      30 #> 5 surprise          15 #> 6 anger             12 #> 7 disgust           11 #> 8 fear              10 #> 9 trust              2

title: Introduction to tidyEmoji
text: Sentiment emotion scoring share one pluggable engine. emoji_lexicons() lists bundled lexicons (plus registered): emoji_score() generic scorer underneath emoji_sentiment(): give data frame emoji column score column — say, scores tailored domain — returns per-row mean, joined codepoint-normalised key everything else: register_emoji_lexicon() stores lexicon name session, can refer emoji_score() — emoji_emotion(), carries emotion columns:
code: emoji_lexicons() #> # A tibble: 2 × 6 #>   name       type      dimensions     n source                           licence #>   <chr>      <chr>     <I<list>>  <int> <chr>                            <chr>   #> 1 novak2015  sentiment <chr [1]>    969 Kralj Novak et al. (2015), PLoS… CC BY-… #> 2 emotag1200 emotion   <chr [8]>    150 Shoeb & de Melo (2020), EMNLP 2… MIT my_lexicon <- data.frame(   emoji = c("\U0001f600", "\U0001f621", "\U0001f637"),   score = c(1, -1, -0.5) )  data.frame(text = c("great \U0001f600", "bad \U0001f621\U0001f637", "none")) %>%   emoji_score(text, lexicon = my_lexicon) #> # A tibble: 3 × 4 #>   text     .emoji_score .emoji_n_scored .emoji_n #>   <chr>           <dbl>           <int>    <int> #> 1 great 😀         1                  1        1 #> 2 bad 😡😷        -0.75               2        2 #> 3 none            NA                 NA        0 register_emoji_lexicon("mine", my_lexicon) emoji_lexicons() %>% filter(name == "mine") #> # A tibble: 1 × 6 #>   name  type   dimensions     n source          licence #>   <chr> <chr>  <I<list>>  <int> <chr>           <chr>   #> 1 mine  custom <chr [1]>      3 user-registered NA

title: Introduction to tidyEmoji
text: emoji appear together? emoji_pairs() returns tidy edge list — one row per pair distinct emoji co-occur entry, number entries . item1/item2/n shape matches widyr::pairwise_count() feeds directly graph tools igraph, tidygraph ggraph: strongest pairings make readable chart :  Set directed = TRUE order pair first appearance, supply doc_id pool several rows (conversation, user, day) one document. emoji_cooccurrence(diagonal = TRUE) additionally returns emoji’s document frequency diagonal. Order also matters within entry. emoji_ngrams() slides window entry’s emoji reading order (text ignored), raw material sequence Markov-style analyses: relational verbs canonicalise glyphs codepoint-normalised key rest package, qualified unqualified forms one emoji count single node.
code: emoji_edges <- ata_tweets %>%   emoji_pairs(full_text)  emoji_edges #> # A tibble: 214 × 3 #>    item1 item2     n #>    <chr> <chr> <int> #>  1 😂    😭       10 #>  2 😂    😩        4 #>  3 😂    🙄        3 #>  4 😭    🙄        3 #>  5 💕    🥰        2 #>  6 💞    🥺        2 #>  7 💯    🤷🏽‍♂️        2 #>  8 🤔    😂        2 #>  9 🤣    😂        2 #> 10 🤷🏼‍♀️    😂        2 #> # ℹ 204 more rows emoji_edges %>%   slice_max(n, n = 10) %>%   mutate(pair = paste(item1, item2),          pair = forcats::fct_reorder(pair, n)) %>%   ggplot(aes(n, pair)) +   geom_col() +   labs(x = "Number of entries containing both", y = NULL,        title = "Emoji that appear together") ata_tweets %>%   emoji_ngrams(full_text) %>%   count(.emoji_ngram, sort = TRUE) #> # A tibble: 159 × 2 #>    .emoji_ngram     n #>    <chr>        <int> #>  1 😂 😂           66 #>  2 😭 😭           27 #>  3 😍 😍           13 #>  4 🥺 🥺           13 #>  5 🤣 🤣           10 #>  6 😭 😂            8 #>  7 🖕🏻 🖕🏻            7 #>  8 😡 😡            5 #>  9 🙏🏾 🙏🏾            5 #> 10 💀 💀            4 #> # ℹ 149 more rows

title: Introduction to tidyEmoji
text: emoji sit much text occupy studied signals right. emoji_position() reports entry’s first last emoji position mean relative position 0 (start) 1 (end):  emoji_density() normalises emoji count text length (per character per whitespace-delimited token), emoji_ratio() reports share text’s characters belong emoji — including .emoji_only flag entries nothing emoji (whitespace):
code: ata_tweets %>%   emoji_position(full_text) %>%   filter(!is.na(.emoji_rel_position)) %>%   ggplot(aes(.emoji_rel_position)) +   geom_histogram(binwidth = 0.05) +   labs(x = "Mean relative position of the entry's emoji",        y = "Number of entries",        title = "Emoji cluster at the end of a message") ata_tweets %>%   emoji_ratio(full_text) %>%   summarise(     n_emoji_only = sum(.emoji_only, na.rm = TRUE),     mean_ratio   = mean(.emoji_ratio[.emoji_ratio > 0], na.rm = TRUE)   ) #> # A tibble: 1 × 2 #>   n_emoji_only mean_ratio #>          <int>      <dbl> #> 1            0     0.0405

text: classification regression work, emoji_dfm() turns corpus document--emoji feature table: one row per entry (per doc_id), one column per emoji, weighted counts, binary presence tf-idf. Every entry kept — emoji-free rows zeros — table binds row--row outcome columns:

what: Translating emoji to and from text
title: Introduction to tidyEmoji
text: Replacing emoji words useful accessibility (screen readers) NLP normalisation step tokenising. emoji_to_text() whole column, either Unicode-name shortcode form; text_to_emoji() inverse. Note qualified heart (carries U+FE0F variation selector) translates just reliably emoji, thanks normalised join key. ad-hoc, vector-level use also as_emoji_name(), as_emoji_shortcode() as_emoji():
code: demo <- data.frame(text = "great \U0001f600 love \u2764\ufe0f") demo %>% emoji_to_text(text, format = "name") #> # A tibble: 1 × 1 #>   text                               #>   <chr>                              #> 1 great grinning face love red heart demo %>% emoji_to_text(text, format = "shortcode") #> # A tibble: 1 × 1 #>   text                          #>   <chr>                         #> 1 great :grinning: love :heart: demo %>%   emoji_to_text(text, format = "shortcode") %>%   text_to_emoji(text) #> # A tibble: 1 × 1 #>   text            #>   <chr>           #> 1 great 😀 love ❤️ as_emoji_name(c("\U0001f600", "\u2764\ufe0f")) #> [1] "grinning face" "red heart" as_emoji_shortcode(c("\U0001f600", "\u2764\ufe0f")) #> [1] "grinning" "heart" as_emoji(c("grinning", "heart")) #> [1] "😀" "❤️"

ℹ 17 more rows emoji_search("celebration") #> # A tibble: 27 × 5 #>    emoji name                     shortcode      group             keyword     #>    <chr> <chr>                    <chr>          <chr>             <chr>       #>  1 🥳    partying face            partying_face  Smileys & Emotion celebration #>  2 🙌    raising hands            raised_hands   People & Body     celebration #>  3 🎅    Santa Claus              santa          People & Body     celebration #>  4 🤶    Mrs. Claus               mrs_claus      People & Body     celebration #>  5 🧑‍🎄    Mx Claus                 mx_claus       People & Body     celebration #>  6 🎂    birthday cake            birthday       Food & Drink      celebration #>  7 🍾    bottle with popping cork champagne      Food & Drink      celebration #>  8 🎃    jack-o-lantern           jack_o_lantern Activities        celebration #>  9 🎄    Christmas tree           christmas_tree Activities        celebration #> 10 🎆    fireworks                fireworks      Activities        celebration #> # title: Introduction to tidyEmoji
text: tidyEmoji ships four datasets, documented help page: emoji_sentiment_lexicon — emoji sentiment scores Emoji Sentiment Ranking (see ?emoji_sentiment_lexicon). emoji_emotion_lexicon — emoji emotion scores EmoTag1200 (see ?emoji_emotion_lexicon). emoji_unicode_crosswalk — one row per emoji name, mapping names / shortcodes glyphs categories. category_unicode_crosswalk — one row per Unicode category, listing emoji. regenerated current Unicode emoji list scripts package’s data-raw/ directory.

what: Discover, Count, Categorise, Score, Translate and Relate Emoji in Text
title: Discover, Count, Categorise, Score, Translate and Relate Emoji in Text
text: tidyEmoji helps discover, count, categorise, sentiment-score, score emotions, translate relate emoji text column — social-media posts, product reviews, chat logs, survey responses, support tickets — summary counts emotion profiles, co-occurrence networks, structural metrics model-ready feature tables, always tidy data frames drop straight tidyverse workflow. Unicode awkward work every code point emoji, makes emoji statistics fiddly. tidyEmoji takes care , including grapheme-aware detection skin-tone modifiers (👍🏽) multi-person sequences (👨‍👩‍👧‍👦) treated single emoji rather split apart.

title: Discover, Count, Categorise, Score, Translate and Relate Emoji in Text

text: emoji_pairs() returns graph-ready edge list emoji appear entry (widyr-style item1/item2/n — pipe igraph, tidygraph ggraph), emoji_ngrams() captures consecutive sequences. structural metrics — emoji_position() (emoji sit end messages), emoji_density() emoji_ratio() (emoji-detection) — emoji_dfm() builds document--emoji count/binary/tf-idf table modelling.

title: tidyEmoji — Roadmap for the Next Release(s)
text: Tidy & composable. verb(data, text, ...) → tibble; unquoted column selection; respect grouped data frames; never force monolithic workflow. Grapheme-aware & correct. Continue delegating detection/extraction emoji::emoji_extract_all() ZWJ sequences skin-tone modifiers stay intact. Don’t reinvent dependency. vector-level re-implementations emoji already exports. Lexicons data provenance. Every bundled lexicon data-raw/ builder, documented source, clear licence. Lexicons pluggable (see §6). Light core, heavy optional. Keep Imports lean; push igraph, ggimage, embeddings, etc. Suggests optional downloads. CRAN-clean & documented. Keep R CMD check green; one focused vignette per major workflow. One canonical join key. (New.) Every glyph → metadata/score join must go codepoint-normalised key (emoji_key()), qualified unqualified forms resolve identically everywhere. 0.2.0 audit (§4) shows happens principle applied inconsistently.

title: tidyEmoji — Roadmap for the Next Release(s)
text: Exported verbs: emoji_summary(), emoji_filter()/emoji_tweets(), emoji_frequency(), top_n_emojis(), emoji_extract_nest(), emoji_extract_unnest(), emoji_tokens(), emoji_categorize(), emoji_sentiment(). Data: emoji_unicode_crosswalk, category_unicode_crosswalk, emoji_sentiment_lexicon (Emoji Sentiment Ranking, valence). Engine: cached one-row-per-glyph reference emoji::emojis; codepoint normalisation robust joins. internals (emoji_reference(), emoji_key(), emoji_glyph_list(), emoji_sentiment_map()) already provide new verbs need — several public verbs bypass emoji_key() join raw glyph; see §4.

title: tidyEmoji — Roadmap for the Next Release(s)
text: emoji_sentiment_map() emoji_sentiment() join glyph → score emoji_key(), strips U+FE0F variation selector qualified text (“❤️”, U+2764 U+FE0F) matches unqualified lexicon entry (“❤”, U+2764). correct even dedicated regression test (tests/testthat/test-sentiment.R:11). every glyph → metadata join uses exact-glyph match emoji::emojis$emoji (crosswalk), without normalisation: R/emoji-extraction.R:67 — emoji_tokens(): match($.emoji, ref$emoji) .emoji_name .emoji_category. R/emoji-summary.R:26 — emoji_frequency(): match(counts$emoji, ref$emoji) name, shortcode, group. R/emoji-categorize.R:20 — emoji_categorize(): setNames(ref$group, ref$emoji) cat_of[g]. R/top-n-emojis.R:68 — top_n_emojis(duplicated = TRUE): inner_join(..., = "unicode") emoji_unicode_crosswalk. bites. existing sentiment test proves extraction yields different qualification form reference/lexicon path needs normalising — otherwise test unnecessary. wherever extracted glyph’s qualification differs emoji::emojis$emoji, exact-match joins return NA even though emoji perfectly well known. Concretely: emoji_tokens() / emoji_frequency(): emoji mismatch get NA name/category still scored sentiment — internally inconsistent output glyph. emoji_categorize(): row whose emoji fail exact match yields empty category set → row dropped entirely ([!.na(cats), ]), even though emoji_summary()/emoji_filter() correctly say contains emoji. top_n_emojis(duplicated = TRUE): inner_join silently drops affected glyph leaderboard. Fix. Build one cached, key-indexed reference (extend emoji_reference() emoji_key column, add emoji_reference_by_key()), route joins — match(emoji_key(x), ref$key) rather match(x, ref$emoji). Add regression tests mirroring sentiment one (qualified glyph must get non-NA name/category survive emoji_categorize()).

what: 4.6 🟠 top_n_emojis() — n semantics and dropped emoji in duplicated mode
title: tidyEmoji — Roadmap for the Next Release(s)
text: R/top-n-emojis.R:78 applies utils::head(, n) many--many join. duplicated = TRUE single glyph occupies several rows, : n counts rows, distinct emoji — top_n_emojis(df, text, n = 20, duplicated = TRUE) can return far fewer 20 distinct emoji. Ties n aren’t broken deterministically, glyph’s multiple-name rows can split across head() cutoff. Glyphs without GitHub alias dropped inner_join duplicated mode, yet kept (shortcode = NA) default mode — inconsistency top 4.1. Fix: decide whether n means “n emoji” “n rows” (recommend: n distinct emoji, take head de-duplicated frequency first, expand names); use left_join (inner_join) alias-less emoji survive; add stable secondary sort key (e.g. glyph) deterministic ties.

what: A. Affect enrichment: emotions + a pluggable lexicon framework
title: tidyEmoji — Roadmap for the Next Release(s)
text: . 0.2.0 scores valence (negative↔︎positive). Research practice want discrete emotions. EmoTag1200 (EMNLP 2020) gives human association ratings 150 popular emoji across 8 Plutchik emotions (anger, anticipation, disgust, fear, joy, sadness, surprise, trust), scores [0, 1], MIT-licensed — easy bundle. Pluggable lexicons. Generalise sentiment machinery small registry valence, emotion, user-supplied lexicons share one mechanism: New dataset: emoji_emotion_lexicon (EmoTag1200, MIT), built data-raw/emoji_emotion_lexicon.R. Effort: M (data + tidy joins reuse emoji_key() — 4.1 fix means join engine already correct). Differentiation EmojiSentR: expose profiles, single composite verdict; combining word-level sentiment stays user’s choice (can document recipe vignette). Optional stretch: valence/arousal/dominance lexicon suitably licensed one exists; opt-emoji_affect() returns valence + emotions together.
code: # 8-emotion profile per row (mean over the row's emoji), tidy & wide emoji_emotion(data, text, lexicon = "emotag1200") #> # adds .emoji_joy, .emoji_trust, ... .emoji_anticipation (+ .emoji_n)  # long form: one row per (row, emotion) emoji_emotion(data, text, long = TRUE)  # the dominant emotion per row, as a convenience emoji_emotion_label(data, text)        #> adds .emoji_emotion (e.g. "joy") emoji_lexicons()                       # list bundled lexicons + metadata emoji_sentiment(data, text, lexicon = "novak2015")   # default emoji_score(data, text, lexicon = my_tbl, by = "emoji")  # bring-your-own

what: C. Relational analysis: co-occurrence, sequences, networks
title: tidyEmoji — Roadmap for the Next Release(s)

None available tidily R. flagship, differentiating capability. title: tidyEmoji — Roadmap for the Next Release(s)
text: . Skin-tone gender modifiers major area social-science research (identity, self-representation, demographic signalling). data fully recoverable codepoints (Fitzpatrick modifiers U+1F3FB–U+1F3FF; gender signs/ZWJ), R tool surfaces . Deps: none (codepoint logic + reference table). Effort: M–L. Sensitivity: document descriptive measures; avoid inferring identity. Cite literature frame carefully vignette.
code: emoji_skin_tone(data, text)            # per emoji: none / light / ... / dark #> uses the 5 Fitzpatrick modifiers; "default" when unmodified  emoji_gender(data, text)               # woman / man / neutral where applicable  emoji_components(data, text)           # decompose a ZWJ sequence into parts #>  "\U0001F468‍\U0001F469‍\U0001F467‍\U0001F466" -> man, woman, girl, boy  # corpus/document diversity indices emoji_diversity(data, text, index = c("richness", "shannon", "skin_tone")) #>  lexical richness, Shannon entropy of the emoji distribution, and a #>  skin-tone diversity index for representation studies

text: . Flags ~258 sequences whole emoji category; mapping countries unlocks geographic analysis emoji-bearing text. Regional-indicator pairs map deterministically ISO 3166-1 alpha-2 (offset 127397, confirmed: intToUtf8(utf8ToInt("🇺🇸") - 127397) → "US"), small ISO table supplies names/regions. Note: also handle subdivision flags (England/Scotland/Wales use tag sequences, regional indicators) non-country regional flags (EU, UN) — edge cases simple offset arithmetic cover. Data: compact bundled ISO 3166 table (reuse {ISOcodes} via Suggests). Effort: S–M. Deterministic, well-specified.

title: tidyEmoji — Roadmap for the Next Release(s)
text: . Emoji strong, compact features text classification, turning model-ready matrices manual today. emoji2vec MIT-licensed MB → bundle; ship via small Suggests-gated download helper (emoji_download_embeddings()), à la textdata. Effort: M (dfm) / L (embeddings infra). emoji_dfm() alone high-value, low-risk addition.
code: # document × emoji count matrix (wide tibble or sparse Matrix), tidymodels-ready emoji_dfm(data, doc_id, text, weighting = c("count", "binary", "tfidf"))  # semantic similarity & neighbours via emoji2vec (MIT, 300-dim, all emoji) emoji_similarity(x, y)                 # cosine similarity between glyphs emoji_nearest(x, n = 5)                # nearest emoji in embedding space

title: tidyEmoji — Roadmap for the Next Release(s)
text: 0.2.0’s NEWS.md accurate (§4.12); keep rigour. Conventions: open # tidyEmoji (development version) heading moment work starts; one bullet per user-visible change, grouped New features / Improvements fixes / Breaking changes / Deprecations; describe behaviour change, commit. Draft entries (refine release time — double release checklist, bullet NEWS-worthy change, work isn’t user-visible yet): 0.2.1 — Improvements fixes - Emoji name, shortcode category now resolve codepoint-normalised key sentiment, emoji carrying U+FE0F variation selector longer get NA metadata, longer dropped emoji_categorize(), longer disappear top_n_emojis(duplicated =   TRUE). (§4.1) - whole package now agrees “contains emoji” means: emoji_summary()/emoji_filter() use detection extraction verbs. (§4.2) - emoji_sentiment() gains .emoji_n_scored (emoji actually found lexicon), distinct .emoji_n. (§4.7) - top_n_emojis(n =) counts distinct emoji, breaks ties deterministically, keeps emoji GitHub-style alias. (§4.6) - Faster large corpora: codepoint keys computed unique glyph set rather per row. (§4.10) - Deprecations: grouped data frames passed emoji_summary(), emoji_frequency() top_n_emojis() now warn grouping ignored (per-group results land 1.0). (§4.3) - Docs: lifecycle badge → “maturing”; vignette sample renamed ata_tweets.csv downsampled (CSV misnamed .rda). (§4.8, §4.12) - (Optional, soft-deprecation — may slip 0.3.0): emoji_summary() columns renamed n_with_emoji/n_total; emoji_tweets() soft-deprecated favour emoji_filter(). (§4.4) 0.3.0 — New features: emoji_emotion()/emoji_emotion_label() + bundled emoji_emotion_lexicon (EmoTag1200); pluggable lexicon API (emoji_lexicons(), register_emoji_lexicon(), emoji_score()); emoji_to_text()/text_to_emoji() + as_emoji*() helpers; emoji_search(). 0.4.0 — New features: emoji_pairs()/emoji_cooccurrence()/emoji_ngrams(); emoji_position()/emoji_density()/emoji_ratio(); emoji_dfm(). 0.5.0 — New features: emoji_skin_tone()/emoji_gender()/emoji_components()/emoji_diversity(); emoji_flag_to_country()/country_to_flag()/emoji_is_flag()/emoji_flags() + emoji_country data. 0.6.0 — New features: emoji_image()/emoji_label()/geom_emoji(); emoji_version()/emoji_vendor_support()/emoji_lookup(); optional emoji_similarity()/emoji_nearest() via emoji_download_embeddings(). 1.0.0 — Breaking changes / Improvements: grouped-df support across verbs; finalise column-name migrations (drop soft-deprecated aliases); lifecycle → stable; performance backend.

title: tidyEmoji — Roadmap for the Next Release(s)
text: fresh read-freshly written verbs surfaced crop bugs — worth recording pattern guard future features: Locale-dependent output. emoji_to_text(format = "shortcode") delegated emoji::emoji_replace_name(), picks alias via rev(sort(...)) — chosen shortcode changed collation locale (testthat’s C locale picked grinning_face; interactive session picked grinning). Fixed always emitting canonical first alias, one emoji_frequency()/as_emoji_shortcode() report. Lesson: never let output depend sort() locale-collated character vector. Dead argument. wrap template applied gsub("\\{x\\}", ..., fixed = TRUE) — regex-escaped pattern fixed = TRUE matches nothing, wrap silently ignored. Lesson: fixed = TRUE patterns must literal; add test per argument. Regex injection. emoji_search("(") errored emoji_search("+1") (real GitHub alias) mis-matched — query used regex. Fixed literal matching lower-cased text. Unreachable dispatch branch. emoji_emotion(lexicon = <registered>) rejected every custom lexicon: registry returns list(type = "custom", tbl = ...), failed .data.frame() test. Also fixed single-emotion matrix collapse (vapply returns vector length-1) emoji_emotion_label()’s hard-coded 8 columns. NA/unknown handling. emoji_to_text()/text_to_emoji() turned NA ""/"NA", unknown glyphs deleted text; NAs now stay NA unknown glyphs stay place. Non-determinism margin. emoji_frequency() tie order depended input order; ties now break glyph (completes §4.6’s determinism). Registered lexicons = "emoji" didn’t resolve emoji_sentiment()/emoji_emotion() (fixed via stored key column), emoji_lexicons() listed glyph/key columns among custom lexicon’s dimensions.

what: 12.3 0.4.0-phase design decisions (locked at implementation; shipping in the 0.3.0 release)
title: tidyEmoji — Roadmap for the Next Release(s)
text: §5C/§5F/§5H proposals left semantics open; decisions implementation locked, : Canonical glyph identity. four corpus-level verbs (emoji_pairs(), emoji_cooccurrence(), emoji_ngrams(), emoji_dfm()) canonicalise extracted glyphs new internal emoji_canonical() (key → reference glyph, pass-unknown), qualified/unqualified twins one node/feature. Empirical fixture: extractor returns bare victory hand U+270C unqualified reference stores U+270C U+FE0F — first --wild instance §4.1 asymmetry can pin test (bare heart U+2764 extracted , ❤/❤️ example §4.1 arise extraction). emoji_frequency() deliberately keeps exact extracted glyph (faithful extraction record); distinction documented. emoji_pairs(): document row, rows sharing doc_id (NA ids form document); pairs distinct emoji (repeats don’t self-pair — diagonal belongs emoji_cooccurrence()); n = number documents containing ; directed = TRUE orders pair first appearance document; output item1/item2/n (widyr-compatible), deterministically sorted. emoji_cooccurrence(diagonal = TRUE): diagonal document frequency (rows item1 == item2), .e. diagonal co-occurrence matrix. emoji_ngrams(): windows slide row’s emoji sequence reading order (intervening text ignored, repeats kept); doc_id (sequence order across rows document meaningful); columns .row_number/.position/.emoji_ngram, glyphs joined sep = " ". Metrics: emoji_position() uses Novak-style relative position (start - 1)/(L - 1) (0 L <= 1), reporting .emoji_first/.emoji_last/.emoji_rel_position; emoji_density() = per character per whitespace-delimited token; emoji_ratio() = share characters (code points) belonging emoji — documented character-based, since base R lacks grapheme segmentation adding {stringi} break light-core principle — plus .emoji_only. Zero-emoji rows get 0 densities/ratio (NA); NA/empty text gets NA. emoji_dfm(): signature (data, text, doc_id = NULL, weighting) — §5H’s (data, doc_id, text) — preserve §2.1 verb(data, text, ...) convention; default document column .row_number (consistent emoji_extract_unnest()), supplied doc_id keeps column name; documents kept (zero rows included) matrix binds row--row outcome columns; wide tibble, sparse Matrix (new hard dependency — sparse/quanteda bridges can come later, §12.4); tfidf = count * log(N / df); columns ordered total count glyph. {rlang} moved Suggests → Imports. Needed correct tidy-eval capture optional doc_id argument (enquo()/quo_is_null() forwarding); already hard transitive dependency via dplyr lifecycle, real dependency weight unchanged. Grouped input emoji_pairs()/emoji_cooccurrence()/emoji_dfm() warns (§4.3 pattern); doc_id supported way express per-group structure 1.0’s grouped-df guarantees.

title: Document-by-emoji feature matrix — emoji_dfm
text: default every row data document first output column, .row_number, position data (matching emoji_extract_unnest()). Give doc_id aggregate rows sharing id one document; id column keeps name. Emoji columns named glyph , canonicalised package's codepoint key (qualified unqualified forms count one feature), ordered descending total count (ties broken glyph). weighting = "tfidf", cell emoji e document d count(d, e) * log(N / df(e)), N number documents df(e) number documents containing e. emoji appears every document therefore scores 0.

title: Document-by-emoji feature matrix — emoji_dfm
code: df <- data.frame(text = c("\U0001f600\U0001f600 fun", "\U0001f621",                           "no emoji")) emoji_dfm(df, text) #> # A tibble: 3 × 3 #>   .row_number  `😀`  `😡` #>         <int> <int> <int> #> 1           1     2     0 #> 2           2     0     1 #> 3           3     0     0 emoji_dfm(df, text, weighting = "binary") #> # A tibble: 3 × 3 #>   .row_number  `😀`  `😡` #>         <int> <dbl> <dbl> #> 1           1     1     0 #> 2           2     0     1 #> 3           3     0     0 emoji_dfm(df, text, weighting = "tfidf") #> # A tibble: 3 × 3 #>   .row_number  `😀`  `😡` #>         <int> <dbl> <dbl> #> 1           1  2.20  0    #> 2           2  0     1.10 #> 3           3  0     0

Either string naming bundled lexicon ("emotag1200", default), name registered lexicon (see register_emoji_lexicon()), data frame. custom lexicon must emoji column one column per emotion (subset eight Plutchik emotions); joined codepoint-normalised key bundled one. long TRUE, return one row per (row, emotion) long form columns .emoji_emotion (emotion name) .emoji_score (mean). text: emoji_pairs() returns tidy edge list emoji appear together document: one row per pair number documents pair co-occurs. default every row data document; give doc_id treat rows sharing id (conversation, user, day) one document. output mirrors widyr::pairwise_count() (item1, item2, n) pipes straight igraph::graph_from_data_frame(), tidygraph ggraph.

title: Co-occurring emoji pairs — emoji_pairs
text: data data frame tibble containing text column. text text column scan, supplied unquoted. doc_id Optional unquoted column identifying documents. Rows sharing value treated one document. Default: row document. directed TRUE, pairs ordered first appearance: document tears--joy emoji appears heart-eyes emoji counts towards (tears--joy, heart-eyes), reverse. Default FALSE (unordered pairs, item1 sorted item2). sort TRUE (default), sort descending n (ties broken item1, item2 order deterministic).

what: tidyEmoji: Discover, Count, Categorise, Score, Translate and Relate Emoji in Text — tidyEmoji-package
title: tidyEmoji: Discover, Count, Categorise, Score, Translate and Relate Emoji in Text — tidyEmoji-package
text: tidy toolkit working emoji text column, social-media posts, product reviews, chat logs survey responses. Unicode awkward handle every code point emoji, makes emoji statistics fiddly obtain. 'tidyEmoji' extracts, counts, categorises, sentiment-scores emotion-scores emoji, converts text (accessibility NLP preprocessing), searches emoji catalogue, maps emoji co-occurrence sequences (graph-ready edge lists n-grams), measures densely emoji used, builds document--emoji feature tables machine learning, grapheme-aware detection (skin-tone multi-person sequences stay intact), returning tidy data frames slot straight 'tidyverse' workflow. bundled emoji sentiment lexicon Emoji Sentiment Ranking Kralj Novak et al. (2015) doi:10.1371/journal.pone.0144296 , released CC -SA 4.0; emotion lexicon EmoTag1200 Shoeb & de Melo (2020) https://aclanthology.org/2020.emnlp-main.720/, released MIT licence.

title: tidyEmoji: Discover, Count, Categorise, Score, Translate and Relate Emoji in Text — tidyEmoji-package
text: Every verb follows verb(data, text, ...), takes text column unquoted, returns tibble. Columns added data carry dotted .emoji_* prefix (.emoji, .emoji_name, .emoji_category, .emoji_sentiment, .emoji_n, ...) collide columns; new summary tibbles (e.g. emoji_frequency()) use bare names. group always refers Unicode top-level category (term used underlying emoji::emojis table). Every glyph--metadata join normalised codepoint key strips U+FE0F variation selector, qualified unqualified emoji forms resolve identically every verb.

title: tidyEmoji 0.3.0
text: emoji_emotion() scores row’s emoji across eight Plutchik emotions (anger, anticipation, disgust, fear, joy, sadness, surprise, trust), using new bundled emoji_emotion_lexicon (EmoTag1200, Shoeb & de Melo 2020, MIT). Supports long form (long = TRUE) one row per (row, emotion). emoji_emotion_label() adds dominant emotion per row. pluggable lexicon API: emoji_lexicons() lists bundled registered lexicons, register_emoji_lexicon() adds , emoji_score() generic scorer verbs share. emoji_sentiment() gains lexicon argument (default "novak2015", unchanged behaviour). Relational analysis. emoji_pairs() returns tidy, graph-ready edge list (item1, item2, n) emoji co-occur document — row document, supply doc_id pool rows — directed = TRUE order pairs first appearance. emoji_cooccurrence() optional diagonal (emoji’s document frequency). emoji_ngrams() slides window row’s emoji reading order returns one row per consecutive n-gram. Structural metrics. emoji_position() reports emoji sit text (first/last character position mean relative position [0, 1]), emoji_density() reports emoji per character per token, emoji_ratio() reports share text’s characters emoji plus .emoji_only flag. emoji_dfm() builds document--emoji feature table (weightings: counts, binary, tf-idf), keeping every document — including emoji-free ones — result binds row--row outcome columns modelling workflows. corpus-level verbs (emoji_pairs(), emoji_cooccurrence(), emoji_ngrams(), emoji_dfm()) canonicalise glyphs package’s codepoint key, qualified unqualified forms emoji (example victory hand without U+FE0F) count one node/feature. emoji_frequency() intentionally still reports exact extracted glyph. emoji_to_text() replaces emoji text column Unicode names shortcodes (demojize — useful accessibility NLP preprocessing), text_to_emoji() inverse (emojize). Vector helpers as_emoji_name(), as_emoji_shortcode() as_emoji() ad-hoc conversion. emoji_search() finds emoji keyword, name shortcode returns tidy tibble matches. New bundled dataset emoji_emotion_lexicon.

title: tidyEmoji 0.3.0
text: emoji_search() matches literally, queries containing regex metacharacters (example +1 alias) safe error. emoji_to_text(format = "shortcode") now always emits emoji’s canonical (first) GitHub-style alias — one reported emoji_frequency() as_emoji_shortcode() — wrap template honoured. Emoji known name/shortcode left place rather dropped text. emoji_to_text() text_to_emoji() keep NA text entries NA. emoji_emotion() emoji_emotion_label() accept registered data-frame emotion lexicons (subset eight Plutchik dimensions), just bundled "emotag1200". Registered lexicons resolve stored normalised key, register_emoji_lexicon(= ) works glyph column name emoji_sentiment() emoji_emotion(). emoji_frequency() (therefore top_n_emojis()) breaks count ties glyph, making output order deterministic. emoji_lexicons() longer lists custom lexicon’s glyph/key columns among score dimensions. package help page (?tidyEmoji) documents output naming contract shared verbs. rlang moved Suggests Imports (tidy-eval capture new optional doc_id argument); already hard transitive dependency, installed footprint unchanged. Grouped data frames passed emoji_pairs(), emoji_cooccurrence() emoji_dfm() warn grouping ignored — use doc_id express per-group structure. vignette header longer carries build date. DESCRIPTION Title Description broadened cover emotions, translation, search, co-occurrence, structural metrics feature tables; version bumped 0.3.0.

title: tidyEmoji 0.2.1
text: Emoji name, shortcode category now resolve codepoint-normalised key sentiment, emoji carrying U+FE0F variation selector longer get NA metadata, longer dropped emoji_categorize(), longer disappear top_n_emojis(duplicated = TRUE). whole package now agrees “contains emoji” means: emoji_summary() emoji_filter() use detection extraction verbs. emoji_sentiment() gains .emoji_n_scored (emoji actually found lexicon), distinct .emoji_n. top_n_emojis(n =) counts distinct emoji rather rows, breaks ties deterministically, keeps emoji GitHub-style alias, preserves exact extracted glyph duplicated mode (one row per distinct alias; left_join instead inner_join). emoji_extract_unnest() now uses .row_number (dotted) avoid collision user columns dplyr::row_number. emoji_summary() column names renamed emoji_tweets/total_tweets n_with_emoji/n_total. old names longer available release. emoji_tweets() soft-deprecated favour emoji_filter(). Faster large corpora: codepoint keys computed unique glyph set rather per row emoji_sentiment() emoji_categorize(). Grouped data frames passed emoji_summary(), emoji_frequency() top_n_emojis() now warn grouping ignored (per-group results land 1.0). Lifecycle badge downgraded stable maturing. Vignette sample renamed ata_tweets.rda (CSV misnamed .rda) ata_tweets.csv downsampled 10k 2k rows. Vignette language updated less Twitter-specific. Crosswalk datasets rebuilt key column normalised joins.

title: tidyEmoji 0.2.0
text: CRAN release: 2026-06-17 tidyEmoji now positioned general toolkit emoji text column (social-media posts, reviews, chat logs, survey responses, …), just tweets.

title: tidyEmoji 0.2.0
text: emoji_sentiment() scores emoji row using bundled emoji_sentiment_lexicon (Emoji Sentiment Ranking Kralj Novak et al., 2015), returning mean sentiment [-1, 1]. emoji_frequency() returns count every emoji text column, name, shortcode category. top_n_emojis() now thin wrapper . emoji_tokens() expands data one row per emoji occurrence name, category sentiment score — tidy, “one-token-per-row” shape. emoji_filter() clearer, text-agnostic name emoji_tweets() (kept synonym). New bundled dataset emoji_sentiment_lexicon.

title: tidyEmoji 0.2.0
text: Grapheme-aware detection. Extraction now keeps skin-tone modifiers zero-width-joiner sequences intact. Previously family emoji (👨‍👩‍👧‍👦) miscounted four separate people skin-tone thumbs-split two “emoji”; now counted one. Much faster. Detection counting longer build multi-thousand-way regular expression every call scan text per known emoji; top_n_emojis() particular dramatically faster large inputs. top_n_emojis() longer emits many--many join warning, reports emoji’s canonical shortcode (e.g. mask) default. verbs now return tibbles consistently (emoji_tweets() previously returned plain data frame), emoji_extract_unnest() longer prints grouping message. Bundled emoji data refreshed current Unicode emoji list (via data-raw/).

title: tidyEmoji 0.2.0
text: Arguments renamed tweet_tbl -> data tweet_text -> text. Code passed positionally (e.g. df %>% emoji_summary(text_col)) unaffected; update calls named old arguments. top_n_emojis(duplicated_unicode = "yes"/"") deprecated favour logical duplicated = TRUE/FALSE. old argument still works warning.
